'''''Galaxy Guide 7: Mos Eisley''''' is a sourcebook written by [[Martin Wixted]] for [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]], published by [[West End Games]] in [[1993]]. Is the first of the [[Galaxy Guide]] series originally published after the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|Second Edition Rulebook]].

''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'' already portrayed characters seen in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] during [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], and ''[[Tatooine Manhunt]]'' gave a first sketch of the city beyond the film. This guide presents a deep view of the city after the events of ''A New Hope'', featuring descriptions and RPG stats for characters, vehicles, and droids. Plus maps of the city, region, and several buildings.

==Publisher's summary==
''Come explore the infamous pirate spaceport city of Mos Eisley. Pounded by the relentless twin suns of Tatooine, Mos Eisley is host to some of the most famous (or infamous) smugglers and criminals in the galaxy!''

''Made famous by the exploits of Han Solo and the treachery of Jabba the Hutt, Mos Eisley is the ultimate adventuring locale for fans of the Star Wars movies.''

''This book provides a complete overview of this outlaw city. It contains detailed location descriptions, with maps, gamemaster character descriptions and illustration. Includes introductory chapters describing the history of Tatooine and Mos Eisley!''

==Continuity==
''Galaxy Guide 7: Mos Eisley'' presents a [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] dealer called [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]. At the time the guide was written, it was just a forgotten name from the early ''Star Wars'' scripts. [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11]] later showed that the character's full name was [[Macemillian-winduarté]]. ''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' adds another connection by showing that the Jedi Mace Windu won the [[Battle of Skor II]] in 21 BBY and became a Squib hero; thus the Mos Eisley Squib could be named for the Jedi.
